counterread_json.verdict: Partial confirmation with corrections and cautions
confirmed_facts: Sakiyama et al. 2017: URAT1/SLC22A12 W258X (rs121907892) and R90H (rs121907896) are loss-of-function variants causing renal hypouricemia type 1.
Sakiyama et al. 2017: In 1,993 Japanese male gout patients, no gout patients carried W258X/R90H.
Sakiyama et al. 2017: The variants were protective against hyperuricemia (P=0.016, OR=0.80).
Yoshioka et al. 2004: Three renal hypouricemia cases had serum UA under 1.0 mg/dL; one W258X homozygote and two sibling heterozygotes.
Ichida 2024 review: ABCG2 participates in urate secretion; reduced-function ABCG2 SNPs are common in Japanese and can cause hyperuricemia via reduced extrarenal excretion.
Ichida 2024 review: Q126X frequency about 3%, Q141K about 32%; Q126X abolishes transport and Q141K halves transport.
Ichida 2024 review: URAT1/SLC22A12 W258X/c.G774A accounts for about 70% of Japanese renal hypouricemia mutations.
Ichida 2024 review: Exercise-induced AKI occurs in about 6-7% of renal hypouricemia patients.
corrections_or_cautions: Sakiyama et al. 2017: The study included 4,902 health-check participants (3,305 males, 1,597 females), not just males. The serum urate reduction values (6.2 to 4.0/0.8 mg/dL in males and 4.5 to 3.5/0.6 mg/dL in females) are confirmed but should be noted as averages.
Yoshioka et al. 2004: The authors caution that whether heterozygous W258X alone can cause renal hypouricemia requires further investigation. This nuance is critical for interpretation.

Ichida 2024 review: The allele frequency of c.G774A is 2.23-2.55% in Japanese, 0.9-1.4% in Koreans, and markedly lower in China. This detail is confirmed but should be contextualized as regional variations.
wiki_safe_claims: URAT1/SLC22A12 W258X and R90H are loss-of-function variants associated with renal hypouricemia type 1.
ABCG2 is involved in urate secretion, and reduced-function SNPs in ABCG2 are linked to hyperuricemia in Japanese populations.
Exercise-induced acute kidney injury occurs in approximately 6-7% of renal hypouricemia patients.
not_safe_to_claim: The exact mechanism by which heterozygous W258X alone causes renal hypouricemia is fully understood.
The regional allele frequency differences of c.G774A are definitively explained by founder effects without further evidence.
The serum urate reduction values in Sakiyama et al. 2017 apply universally to all populations beyond the studied Japanese cohort.
